safeToRun: Reads local launch contracts and public Cloudflare documentation citations. It does not read, request, set, store, or print token values; it does not call Cloudflare, deploy, mutate DNS, create resources, or call paid APIs.

cloudflareApiToken.scopeBoundary: Restrict to the Cloudflare account that will host herbalisti.com and the Herbalisti Workers/Pages resources.

coveredCommands: npx wrangler pages project create herbalisti --production-branch main
npx wrangler pages deploy dist --project-name herbalisti
npx wrangler pages secret put KIE_API_KEY --project-name herbalisti
npx wrangler pages secret put MEDIA_ADMIN_TOKEN --project-name herbalisti
npx wrangler d1 migrations apply herbalisti --remote
npx wrangler secret put FEED_ADMIN_TOKEN --config wrangler.news.toml
npx wrangler secret list --config wrangler.news.toml --format json
Wrangler account discovery in non-interactive CI
npx wrangler r2 bucket create herbalisti-media
optionalPermissions.notes: Only needed when the optional reviewed-video R2 bucket is created.

explicitlyNotRequired.reason: The guarded GitHub workflow does not mutate DNS. herbalisti.com custom-domain and DNS work remains an explicit separate launch step.
The production workflow does not need billing access.

cloudflareApiToken.valueHandling: Create or edit the token in Cloudflare and enter the token value directly into the GitHub production environment secret named CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN. Do not paste the value into chat, docs, Git, screenshots, or logs.

sourceEvidence.title: Cloudflare Pages direct upload with Wrangler
supports: Pages Wrangler deploys require CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID and CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN in CI.
The Pages deployment token needs Account / Cloudflare Pages / Edit permission.

sourceEvidence.title: Cloudflare Pages REST API token guidance
supports: Custom Cloudflare tokens for Pages automation need the Cloudflare Pages permission with Edit access.
Pages API requests accept a Cloudflare API token in the authorization header.

sourceEvidence.title: Cloudflare Workers GitHub Actions authentication
supports: Non-interactive Wrangler CI uses CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN and CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID.
Cloudflare recommends scoping deployment tokens down to the account and zone being deployed.

sourceEvidence.title: Cloudflare Workers build-token permission set
supports: Workers build tokens use Account Settings Read, Workers Scripts Edit, Workers KV Storage Edit, Workers R2 Storage Edit, zone Workers Routes Edit, User Details Read, and Memberships Read.
Herbalisti needs Workers Scripts Edit for scheduled Worker deploy and secret operations; R2 is optional until reviewed video assets exist.

sourceEvidence.title: Cloudflare D1 REST import token guidance
supports: D1 write automation needs Account / D1 / Edit permission.

sourceEvidence.title: Cloudflare API token permissions reference
supports: Cloudflare Pages Edit can create, edit, and delete Pages projects.
D1 Edit grants write access to D1.
Workers Scripts Edit grants write access to Workers scripts.
Workers R2 Storage Edit grants write access to R2 storage.
User Details Read and Memberships Read are user-scoped read permissions.

checks.detail: Guarded workflow reads CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN from GitHub production environment secrets.
Guarded workflow reads CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID from a GitHub production environment variable with secret fallback.
Required Cloudflare token permission packet covers Pages, D1, Workers Scripts, and Account Settings.
Required Cloudflare token permissions map to commands used by the launch workflow or contract.
Optional R2 permission remains separate from launch-critical permissions.
Guarded workflow does not require DNS edit permission.
Token requirement inputs do not contain obvious secret values.
Cloudflare token requirement packet links only to Cloudflare documentation sources.
Deployment runbook and launch packet reference the token requirement gate.
Cloudflare token requirement verifier is exposed as an npm script.
